title: T-017 — Outbox event extensions + replication queue schema (§12.3, §10A.4, D-MS-12)
prompt:
# T-017 — Outbox event extensions + replication queue schema

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §12.3 (TO outbox events), §10A.4 (lane outbox events), D-MS-12
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register the multi-site outbox event types in the central event_catalog (owned by foundation T-111 baseline) and create the operational tables backing the replication queue UI (MS-106 modal, MS-111 master data sync, MS-112 replication queue).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0050_ms_outbox_and_replication.sql`:
   - INSERT INTO event_catalog 5 event types with JSON schema per §12.3 + §10A.4: `transfer_order.shipped`, `transfer_order.in_transit`, `transfer_order.received`, `transport_lane.created`, `transport_lane_rate_card.activated`. Each row has payload_schema JSONB describing required keys.
   - CREATE TABLE replication_jobs(id, org_id, scope CHECK ('all','failed','single'), entity_type, entity_id NULL, status CHECK ('pending','running','completed','failed','retrying'), priority CHECK ('normal','high') DEFAULT 'normal', error_message, attempts INT DEFAULT 0, max_attempts INT DEFAULT 5, scheduled_at, started_at, completed_at, created_by). RLS org-scoped.
   - CREATE TABLE replication_schedules(id, org_id, entity_type, cadence_cron, last_run_at, next_run_at, active). RLS org-scoped.
   - CREATE TABLE replication_conflicts(id, org_id, site_id, entity_type, entity_id, field_diffs JSONB, detected_at, resolved_at NULL, resolved_by NULL). RLS site-scoped via app.current_site_id().
   - Indexes: idx_jobs_active ON replication_jobs(org_id, status) WHERE status IN ('pending','running','retrying'); idx_conflicts_unresolved ON replication_conflicts(org_id, site_id) WHERE resolved_at IS NULL.
2. Drizzle schemas + zod for all 3 tables; event_catalog modifications via SQL only (table owned by foundation).
3. Tests: payload_schema parse-and-validate each new event type via zod-from-jsonschema; replication_jobs CHECK constraint; replication_conflicts RLS site-scoped isolation.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0050_ms_outbox_and_replication.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/replication-jobs.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/replication-schedules.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/replication-conflicts.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/ms-replication.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0050 applied, when event_catalog queried, then 5 new rows exist with the 5 event types and each has non-empty payload_schema JSONB.
2. Given a transfer_order.shipped payload missing 'from_site_id' is validated against payload_schema, when validate runs, then it rejects with required-key error.
3. Given replication_jobs status='unknown' is inserted, when committed, then DB raises check_violation.
4. Given two sites X and Y each have an unresolved replication_conflict, when app_user under withSiteContext(siteId=X) queries replication_conflicts WHERE resolved_at IS NULL, then only X's row returned (RLS site-scoped).
5. Given idx_jobs_active is inspected via pg_indexes, when listed, then partial WHERE clause references the 3 active statuses.

## Test strategy
- RED: assert 5 event_catalog rows + payload_schema validation + replication_jobs CHECK + RLS site-scoped isolation + partial-index WHERE before migration applied.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- ms-replication'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not modify event_catalog rows for events owned by other modules.
- Do not let replication_conflicts be org-scoped — conflicts are inherently per-site.
- Do not allow status='completed' to remain in active-job partial index (would never be reaped).
- Do not skip RLS+FORCE RLS on any of the 3 tables.
- Do not let payload_schema be free-form text — JSON Schema validates payloads downstream.

out_of_scope: Do not implement worker handlers for these events (consumers live in 10-FIN/12-REP/05-WH per their consumer specs).
Do not implement retry/run-sync UI here (T-019).
Do not implement conflict resolution UI (T-018).

cross_module_dependencies.reason: event_catalog table is owned by foundation outbox baseline; this task inserts rows.

Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; replication_conflicts uses app.current_site_id() site-scoped RLS.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0050 applied, when event_catalog queried, then 5 new rows exist with the 5 event types and each has non-empty payload_schema JSONB.
Given a transfer_order.shipped payload missing 'from_site_id' validated against payload_schema, when validate runs, then rejects with required-key error.

Given replication_jobs status='unknown' inserted, when committed, then DB raises check_violation; AND given idx_jobs_active inspected when listed then partial WHERE clause references 3 active statuses.
Given two sites X and Y each have unresolved replication_conflict, when app_user under withSiteContext(siteId=X) queries WHERE resolved_at IS NULL, then only X's row returned (RLS site-scoped).

Do not org-scope replication_conflicts — inherently per-site.
